subject: (no subject)
body:
Dearest Jeffrey,
I'm working for a normal, organized investment bank and I've started dating
a normal guy. He's my age and a management consultant from Croatia. Normal
life is underrated. I might feel borderline happy  :)

*My diary for this week:*

As I'm driving on Sheikh Zayed Road with walls covered with purple,
neon-palm trees and green, neon triangles; and you can see the Fairmont
Hotel lit up in changing colors, you just gotta love Dubai! We are tacky and
we make no excuses for it!

*MAC Capital
*First day at work and my coworker is showing me the filing system. Amongst
a thousand files she randomly pulls out a file on my ex's father...

There are some things in life you shouldn't bother fighting. This is one of
them. I want my ex out of my life so I can move on. He wants to stay
friends. He's not trying very hard, but somehow he just remains in my life.
As pathetic as he may be, I'm glad he's around.

I feel strong and empowered! New job, starting with new people. EVERYTHING
I've achieved in Dubai, I've done on my own. I managed to get all four jobs
on my own and never through anyone else's connections. I pay my own bills. I
do as I please.

*Normal guys*
Dating a normal guy ROCKS! It's INFINITELY better than dating a rich,
CHEAPASS (I'm talking someone who asks you to pick up the bill for him and
his friend...), selfish jerk.

A rich person who is financially cheap, also has a cheap personality.

What touches me with the normal guy I'm trying to date, is that he was there
in my weakest moment: after the car accident. I was in shock and more
"Cecilia" than usual. He called me every few hours to check up on me and
forced me to see a doctor.

I've been suffering from bronchitis since we started dating and he's very
caring.

He's the kind of man you want to find and now that I have, I think I'll have
to work hard on being capable of having a normal relationship. We're going
grocery shopping together for crying out loud!

*Growing up*
After a rocky year in Dubai (4 jobs and 1 shady boyfriend), I'm hoping to
finally have some peace at a normal investment bank (40 employees,
organized, Australian) instead of some crazy Arabic startup run by Lebanese
who shoots from the hip; and dating a normal guy who has respect for himself
and people around him.

*Arabs and warning lights*
When driving home I saw several SUVs with the UAE flag and the warning
blinkers on. What does that mean??? They won a game? They managed to survive
Sheikh Zayed Road? People here use the warning lights more than anywhere
else. During the floods, they urged people via radio not to use the warning
lights. It's bad enough that Dubai is lit up like a christmas tree, I
suppose.

*Survived 1st week at MAC Capital:*
I'm very happy there! Been working like a dog this week. I'm in investment
banking, but we also have brokerage. We're running between the two offices
chitchatting. What happened to the Chinese Wall???

Still suffering from bronchitis and very badly now. My lungs are killing me
after all the coughing. Went to pharmacy since they have some good stuff
here. Hopefully it'll help.

*Girls' dinner*
The Lesbian, the Housewife, the Syrian and the Swede. Talked about politics
in the Middle East.  Well, I have bronchitis, so I was mostly quiet.

Stay well and healthy. Enjoy life. Follow your instincts. Keep good people
around you.
-- 
Cecilia Steen
Dubai Mobile +971 50 940 2062
